user.title: Add a Medication
user.description: Name your medication and choose a tracking mode. Your choice determines which schedule settings appear next.

regular_interval.title: Regular Interval
regular_interval.description: Set up dosing at fixed intervals — e.g. every 8 hours.

data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 2 pills in 8 hours.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar.

time_of_day.title: Time of Day
time_of_day.description: Set up dosing at specific times each day — e.g. twice daily at 8 AM and 8 PM.

data_description.doses_per_day: How many times per day you take this medication. Each time is one entry, regardless of how many pills you take at once. You'll set the exact times on the next screen.
data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 2 pills in 24 hours.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar.

time_of_day_times.title: Dose Times
time_of_day_times.description: Set the time for each daily dose. Times are automatically sorted after saving.

data_description.dose_time_1: Time for the first daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_2: Time for the second daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_3: Time for the third daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_4: Time for the fourth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_5: Time for the fifth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_6: Time for the sixth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_7: Time for the seventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_8: Time for the eighth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_9: Time for the ninth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_10: Time for the tenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_11: Time for the eleventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_12: Time for the twelfth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_13: Time for the thirteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_14: Time for the fourteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_15: Time for the fifteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_16: Time for the sixteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_17: Time for the seventeenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_18: Time for the eighteenth daily dose.

as_needed.title: As Needed
as_needed.description: Set up on-demand dosing with a safety limit. Calendar and adherence tracking are not available for as-needed medications.

data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 2 pills in 8 hours.

cyclic.title: Cyclic/Calendar Pattern
cyclic.description: Set up a cycling schedule — e.g. 5 days on, 2 days off.

data_description.days_on: Number of active days in the cycle.
data_description.days_off: Number of rest days in the cycle.
data_description.cycle_anchor_date: Start date of the on/off cycle.
data_description.dose_time: Time of day to take on active days.
data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 1 pill in 24 hours.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar.

pk.title: Pharmacokinetics
pk.description: Optional: configure to track drug amount in the body over time. Pharmacokinetic information can be found online for most drugs; please use only reputable sources. This tool is for informational and home automation purposes only and is not a certified medical device. Always follow your doctor's advice and the instructions on your prescription. Leave all values at 0 if not needed.

data_description.strength: Amount of medication per dose in mg. Set to 0 if not tracking concentration.
data_description.half_life: Time for the body to eliminate half the drug. Set to 0 if not tracking concentration.
data_description.hours_to_peak: Hours after taking until concentration peaks. Set to 0 for immediate-release medications.
data_description.bioavailability: Percentage of the dose that reaches systemic circulation. Default 100% means no loss. For paracetamol, use 85%.
data_description.lag_time: Minutes before the medication begins releasing. Leave at 0 if unsure — most drugs start releasing immediately. Typical values: 15–30 min for enteric-coated tablets, 60+ min for colon-targeted delivery. (lag time, absorption delay, enteric coating)
data_description.ir_fraction: Percentage of the dose released immediately (immediate release fraction). Only for sustained-release medications. Default 100% means all at once.
data_description.zero_order_duration: Hours the sustained-release phase delivers drug at a constant rate (zero-order release duration). Only for sustained-release medications.
data_description.release_half_life: Time for the remaining drug to be released after the sustained phase ends (dissolution half-life). Only for sustained-release medications.

effectiveness.title: Metrics Tracker & Adherence
effectiveness.description: Select which symptoms to track and configure adherence settings. Each enabled metric adds a 1–10 slider to rate how you feel after each dose.

data_description.custom_metrics: Separate multiple with commas, e.g. brain fog, joint stiffness. A 1–10 slider is created for each.

adherence.name: Adherence Tracking
adherence.description: Monitor how consistently doses are taken on schedule.

data_description.enable_adherence: Show how consistently you take doses on time. Creates 7, 14, 30, and 365-day adherence sensors. Off by default for as-needed medications.
data_description.adherence_grace_hours: How early or late a dose can be and still count as on-time. For example, 1 hour means ±1 hour around the scheduled time. Only applies when adherence tracking is on.

init.title: Schedule & Dosing
init.description: Adjust the schedule and safety limits. Pharmacokinetic and effectiveness settings are on the next pages.

data_description.doses_per_day: How many times per day you take this medication. Each time is one entry, regardless of how many pills you take at once. Save and re-enter options to change the number of time fields.
data_description.dose_time_1: Time for the first daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_2: Time for the second daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_3: Time for the third daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_4: Time for the fourth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_5: Time for the fifth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_6: Time for the sixth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_7: Time for the seventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_8: Time for the eighth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_9: Time for the ninth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_10: Time for the tenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_11: Time for the eleventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_12: Time for the twelfth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_13: Time for the thirteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_14: Time for the fourteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_15: Time for the fifteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_16: Time for the sixteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_17: Time for the seventeenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_18: Time for the eighteenth daily dose.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit.
data_description.days_on: Number of active days in the cycle.
data_description.days_off: Number of rest days in the cycle.
data_description.cycle_anchor_date: Start date of the on/off cycle.
data_description.dose_time: Time of day to take on active days.
data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar. Disabling removes the calendar entity.

options.Regular Interval: Regular Interval — fixed hours between doses
options.Time of Day: Time of Day — one or more times daily at set times
options.As Needed: As Needed — on-demand with a safety limit

options.Instant Release: Instant Release — most drugs, don't change if unsure
options.Sustained Release: Sustained Release — extended-release or bi-layer tablets
